text:
Market today: Tech is still king, but the chip sector is getting hammered. The headlines are all over the place, but what matters for us is anything that hits big tech or semiconductors.

Your holdings: SOXL is still a mess at -28.7%, and its leveraged nature makes it extra painful on margin. QQQI is also on the "TRIM" list, down 1.86%, and needs watching. SPAL is new and already down 9.58% - these leveraged ETFs move fast.

Watch: SMH is showing up on the scanner with a "BUY" signal, which is worth noting given your concentration.

Bottom line: We need to get a handle on those SELL/TRIM signals, especially the leveraged ones.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming or exiting this leveraged position given the -28.7% PnL and inherent decay. No specific stop level provided, but protect capital.
SPAL: Monitor this new leveraged ETF closely. Already down -9.58% on margin, so a tight stop is crucial.
SMH: Scanner shows a "BUY" signal at 586.43-584.71 with a stop at 580.88. If you have conviction, this could be a spot to consider adding or initiating a position, but be mindful of leverage.
